criteria.label: Produce reproducible notebooks verified by Restart & Run All.
criteria.example: Workflow: develop iteratively → Kernel → Restart & Run All → if it passes end-to-end, save and commit; if not, fix the broken cell ordering or missing setup before sharing.

criteria.label: Write small, ordered, idempotent cells free of hidden state.
criteria.example: A cell that does `count += 1` is non-idempotent (running it twice changes state). Prefer `count = compute_count(data)` — running it many times yields the same result and re-execution can't accidentally double-count.

criteria.label: Extract stable logic into tested importable modules.
criteria.example: When `chunk_text(...)` has been copy-pasted across three notebooks, move it to `ragapp/chunker.py` with a unit test. Notebooks `import` it from the module — single source of truth, testable, reusable.

criteria.label: Apply VC hygiene (strip outputs, jupytext) and keep secrets/data out of notebooks.
criteria.example:
```bash
nbstripout --install      # one-time: pre-commit strips outputs automatically
jupytext --set-formats ipynb,py:percent notebook.ipynb   # paired .py for diff-friendly review
```
Secrets via `os.environ` / `userdata.get`, never literal in cells.
